works.author: Paulína Abrahámová
works.title: Cocoon chair
works.text: The Cocoon Chair merges ergonomic seating with wearable design. It creates a personal micro-space by allowing users to “dress” the chair. An integrated vest provides warmth, comfort, and protection in colder conditions, while also functioning as a detachable garment. Without it, the chair reveals upholstery inspired by outdoor winter clothing, suitable for terraces or seasonal markets.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Martina Ďalogová
works.title: Glitch
works.text: The project interprets the Space to Space theme as a generational shift in perception. Glitch, originally a digital phenomenon, is translated into a physical object and serves as a visual metaphor for the divide between generations raised in analog and digital environments. It reflects a moment when established and emerging interpretations of reality no longer align.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Hana Šegedová
works.title: SPOJOVÁ school garden
works.text: The school garden was designed as a link between the school’s identity and children’s imagination. It responds to its environment and the diversity of age groups, creating a space for cooperation and quiet discovery. It encourages learning through touch, movement, and play. Natural materials support authentic contact with the outdoors and enable adaptation to seasonal change.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Hana Šegedová
works.title: UMAMA
works.text: Umama is a furniture piece inspired by the balance, strength, and ingenuity of African women and mothers. It reflects their ability to adapt, take responsibility, and transform everyday tasks into thoughtful, functional actions. The design reinterprets the tradition of carrying loads on the head within a contemporary context, showing how cultural heritage can naturally integrate into modern ways of living and space.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Hana Šegedová
works.title: WARKA house
works.text: The design combines the principle of the Warka Tower with a contemporary interpretation of traditional African housing to address water scarcity. Rainwater collection is integrated into the roof structure, forming a unified system with the architecture and interior. The project uses local materials, respects cultural contexts, and aims to sustainably improve everyday living conditions.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Ema Kmecová
works.title: Face to Face
works.text: Café Face to Face is conceived as a social platform where holographic columns enable interaction across distance. It creates a hybrid space for both physical and digital encounters. The accompanying chair functions as its micro-scale counterpart, with polycarbonate elements revealing bent rods resembling digital wiring. Together, they form an environment where distance transforms into presence and space becomes a bridge between people.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Gréta Brajerová
works.title: Errans
works.text: The Errans project (from the Latin ‘wandering’) is a socio-ecological initiative situated in an underpass, restoring dignity to both homeless people and discarded shopping carts. As a sheltered workshop, it enables individuals to earn a night’s stay by redesigning these carts. The process unfolds through four zones: treatment, purification, rest, and a glass-walled workshop, creating visibility and space for those long overlooked.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Ema Kováčová
works.title: DUENDE
works.text: Duende embodies tension, where silence transforms into passion and stillness into movement. The object expresses the raw language of flamenco, from the fluidity of braceo to the intensity of zapateado before its release. Sharp angles meet softness in a dialogue of masculine and feminine principles. It is not only a seat, but a visual trace of emotion shaped through gesture.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Martina Hatalová
works.title: From Air to Ground
works.text: What if a package, once ordered, arrived directly from the sky? Delivered to its “nest” by a battery-powered carrier pigeon, the concept imagines a new form of aerial logistics. A drone targets a circular landing point to place the package safely, then returns to a birdhouse-like station to recharge before its next flight, redefining delivery as a seamless spatial experience.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Samuel Rusnák
works.title: YTERA
works.text: The project explores the evolving meaning of seating across time. It begins with the ancient Egyptian headrest as an archetypal form of the past, while the present is embodied by YTERA as a static illusion. The future is represented by its eighth generation as a sound frequency, imagining a world where the human body is no longer essential and furniture becomes a distant echo.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Viola Muchová
works.title: Layra
works.text: Layra explores space as a shifting relationship between body, material, and form. Within the Space to Space context, it generates a dynamic environment through physical interaction with layered textile structures that respond through movement and deformation. The seating becomes an active element, transforming its form and visual character, and shaping a new spatial experience between the object and the user.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Alexandre Guezenoc
works.title: How
works.text: The poster series interprets Space to Space as a relationship between physical and mental dimensions. Through intense colours, irony, and metaphors of transition, it confronts cosmic expansion with questions of responsibility, consumerism, and freedom of choice. The works question whether, without a shift in perspective, we are capable of designing empathetic and ethical futures.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Michał Sieczka
works.title: Matches
works.text: This experimental documentary, shot on 16mm film, examines tree felling as a disruption of local memory and the natural rhythm of the landscape. The raw materiality of the analogue medium, with its grain and fragility, becomes a parallel narrative layer that reflects and intensifies the physical transformations unfolding on screen, adding emotional depth to the observed changes.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Jaka Kordiš, Matic Lesjak, Črt Štrubelj
works.title: What remains
works.text: This speculative design project explores contemporary burial practices through transforming blood and hair into a biodegradable composite used to create an urn. The material supports the body’s return to natural cycles of decay and regeneration, while prompting reflection on death, mourning, and the legacy we leave behind within today’s consumer-driven society.
src: ALUO/Jaka Kordiš, Matic Lesjak, Črt Štrubelj_What remains/media/aluo_what-remains_01.webp

works.author: Umihana Dizdarević, Marko Gorenak, Anja Tudjan, Nina Vranješ
works.title: Seapack
works.text: Seapack addresses marine biodiversity and ecosystem health through the development of a biomaterial made from dried seaweed collected on the Slovenian coast. Typically treated as waste, this resource is transformed into a biodegradable and compostable alternative to petroleum-based packaging foams. The project reduces environmental impact while proposing a circular system in which materials derived from the sea can safely return to it.
src: ALUO/Umihana Dizdarević, Marko Gorenak, Anja Tudjan, Nina Vranješ_Seapack/media/ALUO_Seapack_01.webp

works.author: Danna Bernhard
works.title: A Dollhouse
works.text: Tiny Moves explores the transformation of childhood play into a constructed world of imagination. The project presents a modular dollhouse designed as a set for stop-motion filmmaking, where figures are animated through incremental movement. By revealing the materiality of toys while reactivating the illusion of life, it invites viewers to suspend scale and perception, becoming immersed in a world shaped by gesture and narrative.
src: BAAZ/Danna Bernhard_Tiny Moves/media/Tiny Moves by Danna Bernhard - Danna ber (1080p, h264)_web.mp4
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Noa Sade Satat
works.title: Gerem
works.text: Gerem presents a monolithic 3D-printed concrete staircase exploring additive manufacturing in architecture. The technology enables material efficiency, geometric freedom, and design flexibility. The layered printing process creates a woven structural texture, combining simplicity and functionality. The project shows how 3D concrete printing can be applied in domestic space and contemporary architectural design.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Sunny Lustov
works.title: Nereids
works.text: Nereids are underwater structures designed to mitigate rising ocean temperatures and their impact on coral reefs. Made from N-isopropylacrylamide (NIPA), a thermo-responsive gel, they enable continuous active cooling in vulnerable areas. Their adaptive geometry and material integration create protective sanctuaries that blend with the environment, supporting the resilience and long-term survival of coral reef ecosystems.
src: BAAZ/Sunny Lustov_Nereids/media/NEREIDS Exhibition - by Sunny Lustov_1080p_web.mp4
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Ofir Grinboim
works.title: Split identities
works.text: Split Identities explores the tension between civilian life and military service, reflecting a sudden shift in personal identity. The project translates this duality into objects that oscillate between two worlds, carrying traces of both. Through transformation of form, material, and function, familiar objects are reinterpreted, questioning their meaning and revealing new narratives shaped by conflict and transition.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Itai Orion
works.title: Stairwheel
works.text: The project addresses the challenge of transporting patients in staircases where accessibility is limited. Conventional methods require lifting the full weight of the patient, placing strain on emergency crews. The design introduces a soft stretcher suspended above a central wheel, enabling smoother movement. This approach improves patient comfort while significantly reducing physical effort during emergency transport.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Jozef Mikušinec
works.title: Space Simulation
works.text: The project explores how objects influence the path of light, creating distortions of a background image, here represented by a photograph of Mars. Although positioned on a 2D plane, the objects interact through simulated gravitational forces. An adjustable light-bending parameter allows precise control of distortion, resulting in a dynamic visual system that illustrates the complex relationship between mass and light.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Adam Svoboda
works.title: Nebula
works.text: Nebula presents an endlessly evolving cosmic environment generated within Blender’s shader editor. The scene features procedurally created asteroids inspired by early vector graphics, combined with layered noise and shifting colour palettes. These elements interact to create depth and motion, forming a continuously changing composition that highlights both the infinite nature of space and the generative process behind its creation.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Martin Nastoupil
works.title: Resonance in Space
works.text: Resonance in Space explores the intersection of acoustics and art by reinterpreting Chladni’s sound patterns in three-dimensional space. Sound becomes a dynamic spatial phenomenon as participants activate the system through audio input. Particles respond in real time, gathering and dispersing according to frequency and intensity, transforming sound into a tangible visual experience.
src: MUNI/Martin Nastoupil_Resonance in Space/FI MUNI_Nastoupil_Martin_Resonance in Space_web.mp4

works.author: Matúš Talčík
works.title: Darwin’s Cube
works.text: The project reinterprets Conway’s Game of Life into a pseudo-3D environment on the surface of a cube. Instead of an infinite grid, wrapped edges simulate continuity, allowing patterns to evolve seamlessly across faces. It explores complexity, emergence, and how simple rules generate unpredictable behavior, reflecting on boundaries, infinity, and the illusion of the infinite within finite structures.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Patrik Sluka
works.title: Blow Me Away!
works.text: Blow Me Away! is an interactive visualisation of a state map shaped by wind trajectories. By blowing into a microphone, viewers influence the movement of cities and destabilise the system. The project reflects on natural forces, geopolitics, and the fragility of borders, highlighting how a small individual gesture can trigger broader societal change and shift perception of control.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Pavol Šeliga
works.title: AS IF NOTHING HAPPENED
works.text: As If Nothing Happened is a VR experience exploring perception, memory, and transformation. The work guides the viewer through shifting environments where change remains almost imperceptible, evoking uncertainty and disorientation. Structured as a videogame level mod for Half-Life: Alyx, it stages abstract feelings through spatial narrative, inviting reflection on absence, time, and traces of what once mattered.

works.author: Martin Kopera
works.title: Speed of transformation
works.text: The work explores the relationship between humans and machines as a reflection of the present era, where speed, performance, and technology represent both value and threat. It examines the dissolving boundary between the organic and the mechanical, suggesting a shift in societal direction. Speed becomes a metaphor for contemporary life—fascinating yet destabilising the inner balance of both individuals and systems.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Ron Feldman, Felix Stängle, Kateryna Simson
works.title: Lucid Dreams MAV 5786
works.text: MAV 5786 is an interactive installation that places visitors’ emotional states at its core. Acting as an empathetic interface between human and machine, it materialises a personal “Happy Palace” as a space of inner refuge. The system captures emotions and translates them into frequencies, creating a synesthetic environment that blurs the boundary between observer and artwork.
src: USTP/Ron Feldman, Felix Stängle, Kateryna Simson_Lucid Dreams MAV 5786/Media/mav_trailer_exp1_web.mp4

works.author: Adam Čanecký
works.title: Orienting the system
works.text: Today’s world is not just a set of physical places, but a network of interconnected systems we navigate simultaneously. Each has its own logic, rules, and rhythm. Though often invisible, they shape our behavior, decisions, and perception of reality. The poster series explores orientation within biological, mental, digital, and social systems shaping everyday life.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Adam Kratochvíl
works.title: Manual of improper being
works.text: The leporello presents instructions on how to behave in situations where no correct behavior exists. It highlights the paradox of spaces that are not destinations but part of the journey. These places are meant for passage or temporary presence and share a sense of uncertainty—we do not fully know how to behave, as they are neither for staying nor for complete movement.

works.author: Anna Chodurová
works.title: Archive without a Viewer
works.text: The project explores the relationship between reality and its digital capture. Instead of fully experiencing the present, moments are transferred into archives where images accumulate without continued attention. The work reveals the gap between lived experience and its representation, questioning the value of preservation and asking for whom these images exist if they are no longer meaningfully revisited.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Marietta Brandisová
works.title: Between Nature and Technology
works.text: This poster series explores the shifting relationship between the organic world of nature and the synthetic realm of technology. Through animated compositions, it visualises moments where these environments intersect, overlap, and influence one another. The project suggests that technology is not separate from nature, but emerges from its principles, structures, and processes, revealing a continuous dialogue between both domains.

works.author: Zuzana Buňatická
works.title: Upcycled Light Object
works.text: This light object is created through upcycling a lampshade from a broken vintage lamp, transforming a utilitarian element into an expressive piece balancing on the edge of kitsch. The original diffuser is replaced with dichroic film, interacting with daylight to disperse coloured reflections. At night, it becomes an autonomous light source, acting as a dominant focal point within the surrounding space.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Tereza Sedláčková
works.title: Asmo
works.text: Asmo is an indoor climbing structure for bearded dragons that combines 3D printing and wood to support natural movement outside the terrarium. Its elements mimic branches and driftwood, while the textured surfaces enhance grip and mobility. The modular system consists of climbing holds and rods connected by joints, allowing flexible configurations that enrich the animal’s environment and encourage active behaviour.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Pavla Hajná
works.title: Children's Canopy
works.text: Children's Canopy defines a space within a space, separating the child’s world from the adult environment. Inspired by motifs such as a starry night sky, the design evolves with age, shifting from a playful mobile to immersive structures like a bunker or castle. Made from lightweight Vylen foam, it encourages imagination and adaptability, supporting children’s creativity and changing modes of play.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Elizabeth Bugyiová
works.title: interspace
works.text: Interspace connects traditional drawing with digital light in an interactive dialogue between inner and outer worlds. A scratched image on plexiglass responds to the viewer—its illumination intensifies with proximity, creating a subtle animation. Three figures express uncertainty, loneliness, and hope, suggesting that emotions shift through human contact and are shaped by the presence of others.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Matej Masaryk
works.title: Reflections
works.text: Reflections translates physical space into a digital layer using TouchDesigner to generate a real-time point cloud from a camera. Acting as a digital mirror, it presents viewers and their surroundings as abstract data images. The work shifts perception and reveals how technology shapes our understanding of reality, offering an alternative perspective on what is seen, interpreted, and often overlooked.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Šárka Nešporová
works.title: Break the Cycle
works.text: Break the Cycle explores the relationship between space and the human body, focusing on how context shapes perception and experience. The work communicates its message through a deliberately open narrative, allowing space for individual interpretation. Created using TouchDesigner and Premiere Pro, the video invites viewers to reflect on cycles of behaviour and the subtle forces that influence human action.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Theo Fekar
works.title: Testimonies of the Future
works.text: Testimonies of the Future is a web-based project presenting two diaries of adolescents set in different timelines. One follows a boy remaining on Earth after the departure of privileged groups to colonise a new planet, while the other reflects the life of a girl growing up in a controlled environment on that planet. The work explores inequality, separation, and contrasting visions of future existence.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Barbora Gajdošová
works.title: Consume
works.text: Consume presents a set of copper lip jewellery and a ring shaped like a fork, both incorporating onyx elements that complete one another when brought together. The work reflects on overconsumption and the illusion of emptiness that drives material desire. It suggests that this perceived lack is not natural, but constructed, pointing to the influence of external systems shaping human needs and behaviour.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Kateřina Vyroubalová
works.title: Elsewhere
works.text: Elsewhere is a jewellery collection of a ring and pendant made of copper and agates, inspired by the idea of life beyond Earth. The forms evoke spatial structures resembling microorganisms or energy entities rather than human-like figures. The project reflects the tendency to interpret the unknown through familiar patterns, while opening space for imagination and alternative ways of perceiving other worlds.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Michal Grzebinski
works.title: Relic
works.text: Relic explores the concept of transfer between spaces, where a raw stone carries the memory of its origin, shaped without human intervention. In contrast, a geometric structure represents human order and control. The stone is not set harmoniously but constrained within the form, held at minimal points, creating tension between natural processes and constructed systems, and revealing the fragile balance between both.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Sára Timošová
works.title: There was one house
works.text: There Was One House is a ring inspired by the form of a signet, symbolising home as a space of memory and relationships. The house motif reflects what remains hidden behind closed doors and how these experiences shape identity. Archival photographs evoke intimate family moments, centred around a table embedded within a structure of brass, plexiglass, and printed foil, forming a layered narrative of personal history.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Lena Navrátilová
works.title: Jasmina
works.text: Jasmina is an isometric journey into the unconscious that transforms therapeutic processes into an interactive narrative. Through dreamlike metaphors and evidence-based approaches, it guides players in navigating anxiety and depression. As a digital sanctuary, it functions as an empathetic tool for personal growth, offering support, reflection, and clarity even in moments of emotional uncertainty.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Lukáš Kučera
works.title: Sinequo
works.text: Sinequo is a combat-racing game set in a mechanical Renaissance, where horses are replaced by early self-propelled machines. The gameplay centres on switching between immersive 3D racing and a 2D blueprint mode, where the environment becomes a dynamic sketch. This planning dimension allows players to modify their machines in real time, respond to track conditions, and develop strategies during the race.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Matouš Marek
works.title: Revenant Earth 2
works.text: Revenant Earth 2 is a 2D pixel-art strategy game focused on restoring a planet affected by climate change. As the last human, the player controls weather, soil, plant growth, and technology to re-establish balance. The game simulates ecosystem processes and highlights the impact of human activity, encouraging environmental awareness and responsible attitudes toward nature.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Jana Jagošová
works.title: Liferaft
works.text: Liferaft addresses water safety by improving response time and accessibility in emergency situations. Its modular system allows flexible use and adaptation to different environments, while also serving as a resting point for swimmers. The design integrates lifebuoys and an emergency button with a visual beacon, and a lowered section below the water surface facilitates the safe rescue of a person in distress.
src: UTB/Jana Jagošová_Liferaft/media/Tomas Bata University in Zlín_Jagošová_Jana_Vor_02a.webp
UTB/Jana Jagošová_Liferaft/media/Tomas Bata University in Zlín_Jagošová_Jana_Vor_02a_preview.webp

works.author: Jan Kroulík
works.title: MUV Modular Utility Vehicle
works.text: MUV redefines utility mobility as an adaptable platform for future logistics and emergency operations. Its autonomous, symmetrical base enables rapid integration of specialised modules, from cargo transport to rescue systems. Swappable battery packs reduce downtime and support renewable energy use, while all-wheel drive and steering ensure agile movement in both urban environments and challenging off-road conditions.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Gabriela Metzová
works.title: Rescue Robot
works.text: Rescue Robot is designed to locate people in hard-to-reach environments and establish audiovisual contact during emergency situations. Equipped with night vision, thermal imaging, and signal detection systems, it enhances search efficiency. Its tendon-driven mechanism and flexible joints enable movement in multiple directions. The robot can operate via cable for stable transmission or as a battery-powered unit.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Martin Mejvald
works.title: NIAD Filtration System
works.text: NIAD is a portable water filtration system that combines functionality with a refined, durable design. Its rounded form enhances impact resistance while maintaining a clear aesthetic. The system enables filtration from common sources and efficient rainwater collection. Equipped with an adjustable shoulder strap, it offers convenient access to drinking water during travel without occupying space in a backpack.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Jakub Hrdina, Marek Ondřej, Vladimír Vykoukal
works.title: To Mars!!!
works.text: To Mars!!! is a design research experiment exploring the shift from object design to speculative worldbuilding. Using generative AI and video synthesis, it presents an audiovisual study of Martian infrastructure without human presence. The use of a CCTV aesthetic serves as an analytical lens, examining operations in extreme environments while expanding the methodological boundaries of contemporary industrial design.

works.author: Michaela Krčová
works.title: It’s about you
works.text: The project understands space as a state of mind, creating environments for slowing down, silence, and attentive perception of subtle natural details. In contrast to external noise and constant movement, it encourages a shift inward toward the present moment, personal calm, and self-awareness. It offers a quiet pause within everyday life, inviting reflection and a more mindful connection to one’s surroundings.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Filip Huraj
works.title: MARS53
works.text: Mars 53 presents a concept of minimal housing in a colonised Martian environment in the year 2140. Modular dwellings within dome-based settlements respond to extreme conditions and limited resources. The design addresses essential human needs through the use of local materials, 3D printing, and adaptable interiors, supporting movement, resilience, and long-term sustainable living beyond Earth.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Viktória Palúchová
works.title: Y-Domatia
works.text: The project imagines a future where a threatening celestial body (meteor) is reshaped by a precise laser into an upholstered chair. The object fuses chaos and control, nature and human ingenuity, turning an apocalyptic threat into a functional sculpture. In “space to space,” the energy of danger becomes an intimate place of safety—we sit on what once threatened us.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Lujza Novosadová
works.title: Infinity loop
works.text: This project offers self-sufficient ecotourism in the Egyptian desert. Underground architecture minimizes light pollution for elite stargazing. A rooftop system ensures autonomy: collectors inspired by desert lizard gills extract moisture from the air to produce water, while integrated wind turbines produce energy. It provides an authentic, eco-conscious experience focused on desert survival and beauty.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Martin Žabka
works.title: Shinrin
works.text: The seating is inspired by bonsai shaping and the idea of bringing nature closer to people working in busy offices. The aim of the design is to create a quiet and calming place in the middle of the working environment that will serve as an escape from the noise and bustle of the workplace. The green color refers to tree tree canopies and promotes calm and concentration, while the white on the elastic covers symbolizes purity and peace.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Natália Gajdošová
works.title: breO2
works.text: The project presents an autonomous oxygen cell formed by a bio-technological system. Photobioreactor, hydroponic towers, and a diffuser ceiling produce oxygen and shape the space's atmosphere. The flowing design invites short pauses for the user. The stool reflects the towers’ vertical rhythm. The chrome finish lightens the volume, reflects light and movement, creating contrast with the organic matter and technological core.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Monika Koišová
works.title: Spojová Hub
works.text: The project focuses on the revitalization of an underutilized space behind Spojová Primary School in Banská Bystrica (SK). The aim of the design is to create a multifunctional playground that supports movement, play, and relaxation for different age groups. The space connects after-school activities with areas for older students, forming a place for meeting, interaction, and everyday school life.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Kvetoslava Krnáčová
works.title: Space No.01
works.text: The project explores space as a boundary between the known and the unknown. Inspired by a black hole, it presents an experimental interior as a futuristic waiting area before departure to the Moon. The station is not an object but a state where one leaves terrestrial identity and enters a new reality, where waiting becomes a conscious process of transformation.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Ewa Kulewicz
works.title: The Polish Pavilion for World Expo 2025
works.text: The pavilion design is a comprehensive concept integrating architecture, façade, interiors, and the visitor route. Inspired by a tree trunk—its grain and layered structure—it reflects history, identity, and cultural continuity. Wood is the main material, with circulation tunnels carved like termite channels, alongside references to Polish culture and a dialogue with Japan through the “red thread of destiny” installation.

works.author: Caihong Yang
works.title: Orb Stone
works.text: Traditional whetstones have a rough appearance and are often hidden in drawers or corners. They require learned technique and are therefore used only occasionally. If a whetstone becomes an everyday kitchen utensil – present and visible in the kitchen – it is no longer merely a tool, but an object that can be displayed, embodying a lifestyle that integrates maintenance, care, and daily ritual.
src: TAIPEI TECH/Caihong Yang_Orb Stone/media/產品介紹.webp

works.author: Dennis Baptist
works.title: Microcosmos
works.text: Projection mapping and an audiovisual experience transform frames into connected micro-worlds. Visuals continuously evolve as an external force moves between frames, influencing and synchronizing them. This dynamic interplay reveals shifting relationships across different dimensions, showing how environments adapt and connect through motion and change.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Oliveira Tainá
works.title: Space to Space — An Immersive Exhibition
works.text: The project develops a VR experience (Meta Quest 3) exploring communication design as a mediator between physical, social, digital, and conceptual space. An interactive collage-based environment enables intuitive, sensory exploration. It includes a prototype with a central hub and four themed rooms, created using ShapesXR, Figma, and Photoshop, each addressing key human and spatial questions.

works.author: Tiago Sequeira
works.title: The human spirit of space
works.text: This editorial project links architecture and design, viewing space as a reflection of societal values and emotions. It explores the relationship between collective emotion and the built environment, treating architecture as a cultural mirror. Focusing on transitions between physical and social space, it examines historical contexts and emotional impact, developing a visual language that expresses states of mind.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Zheng Meng-Cong
works.title: Ning-Stones
works.text: Ning-Stones enhance sensory perception and reduce everyday stress through a multi-sensory design experience. Crafted from recycled marine glass, the project combines magnetic levitation lighting, wave drums, floating oil stones, massage stones, and aromatherapy elements. By merging sustainable materials with interactive functions, it creates a calming environment that transforms waste into a refined and immersive meditation experience.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Beatriz Martins
works.title: VRSA
works.text: The application is dedicated to the municipality of Vila Real de Santo António (Algarve, Portugal), addressing fragmented information across its three parishes. It organizes and enhances heritage, tourism, and events, promoting clear and integrated communication through an attractive design. It provides access to up-to-date photos of points of interest and an interactive map divided by parishes.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.

works.author: Eva Santos
works.title: FSSolutions
works.text: The project developed a visual identity and digital strategy for a high-tech engineering organization, translating complex technology into a clear, premium, and humanized language. Based on a hexagonal concept and an “F–S” symbol, it combines structure and fluidity. A navy, cyan, and purple palette reinforces trust and innovation. The system includes brand guidelines, print and digital outputs, and an intuitive website.

works.author: David Voznyak
works.title: VIVO
works.text: The project explores audiovisual design, interactivity, and biometrics to support introspection and bodily awareness. It responds to sensory overload by redirecting attention to inner rhythm. Using heart rate data and motion sensors, it generates visuals. The system integrates a heart monitor, Bluetooth, Python, Leap Motion, and TouchDesigner into an interactive installation reacting to the user’s physical state.
warnings: Nebyl nalezen anglický marker (ENG:), použit celý text.
